title: Quick check: Image and font optimization

choices.text: Serves modern formats (WebP, AVIF) to supporting browsers; generates multiple resolutions for retina; lazy-loads off-screen images by default; requires width/height (or fill) to prevent CLS; caches optimized variants. With `priority` for hero images and `sizes` for responsive layouts, it dramatically improves LCP. External images need `remotePatterns` config in next.config.js. Replacing `` with `` is one of the highest-leverage performance wins in Next.js.

questions.explanation: Auto format conversion, multi-resolution, lazy-loading, dimensions enforced.

questions.prompt: Page using Google Fonts via `<link>` has slow LCP and noticeable layout shift on first load. Fix?

choices.text: Switch to `next/font/google` — downloads the font at build time, self-hosts on your domain (no third-party request), inlines critical font CSS (eliminates flash of unstyled text), subsets to characters used (smaller bundle), preloads automatically. `import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'`; specify `subsets: ['latin']` and only the `weight` array you use. Pair with `display: 'swap'` (default) to prevent invisible text. Zero external font requests, zero layout shift from font loading, optimal CWV scores.

questions.explanation: next/font self-hosts, subsets, preloads — eliminates third-party requests and layout shift.
